close_reason: Added shared_client() with OnceLock, removed per-instance client
description:
src/lib.rs:155 'client: reqwest::Client::new()' creates a fresh HTTP client per from_uri. reqwest::Client is internally Arc-shared and meant to be cloned across uses; constructing many is wasteful (each holds its own connection pool, TLS resolver state, DNS cache).

In the umbrella secretx crate's typical usage (one backend per URI, cached by the dispatcher), this is fine. In a hypothetical caller that repeatedly constructs GcpSmBackend (e.g., for token refresh on each call), the construction cost adds up — DNS resolves and TLS handshakes are not shared across backend instances.

Cross-crate aspect: same pattern in all REST backends. Workspace-wide.

Suggested fix: a once-cell'd thread-safe shared client at the crate root, cloned into each instance. Or accept the design as-is and document that long-running daemons should hold one GcpSmBackend per secret URI, not reconstruct.

Low priority because the typical caller pattern likely makes this irrelevant.
title: reqwest::Client created per backend instance; each has its own connection pool
